content: Is dynamic debug somehow not usable with Rust? I have tried to enable dev_dbg!() statements but cannot find them listed in /sys/kernel/debug/dynamic_debug/control...
Seems so, Rust's pr_debug! just call _printk().
Ah, that explains then! Thanks for clarifying, I got tricked by the name
And looks to me that dynamic debug requires static_key
No, I think it's a missing feature ;-)
https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/453

Boqun Feng said:

And looks to me that dynamic debug requires static_key

It also requires the support of __UNIQUE_ID()
We already have static key, and unique id can be replaced with symbol mangling, so it seems doable?

Alice Ryhl said:

We already have static key, and unique id can be replaced with symbol mangling, so it seems doable?

Yes, I'm only waiting for a wild developer to show up :-)
... which brings my next question, in C the usual way to show debug printks for a given module is to define DEBUG somewhere in the code, but how do I do that in Rust... ? :sweat_smile:
I don't think we will ever support that, having dynamic debug printing should be good enough?
Definitely, but I'm trying to find a way to see my debug statements in the meantime
(without triggering them generally in the whole kernel)
a quick hack would be changing the if condition of Device::pr_debug()
you can compare the device name, or try to see if there’s any extra byte you can set in struct device :)

Alexandre Courbot said:

Definitely, but I'm trying to find a way to see my debug statements in the meantime

+1 from me btw, it's either a barrage of debug messages or none.

Could we do filtering of debug/info messages at runtime before handing them to _printk? We could steal the logic from env_filter (used by env_logger) so you could set a config option like RUST_LOG := device=debug,kernel=warn
Or even cooler, store a pointer to the filter in a static and expose some way to pass a new filter at runtime
is there no native filtering in the kernel?

Benno Lossin said:

is there no native filtering in the kernel?

There is https://docs.kernel.org/admin-guide/dynamic-debug-howto.html#dynamic-debug
then we should support that, no?
yep
I think it uses the static key stuff for static branching. We already have some bits and pieces of that in Rust.
If you scroll up a bit, we already discussed supporting dynamic debug and a bit how to do that ;-)

Boqun Feng said:

If you scroll up a bit, we already discussed supporting dynamic debug and a bit how to do that ;-)

Yes, we can go in circles for years like this. My context window is not that long :laughter_tears:

I got nerd sniped on this.
I have a somewhat working demo. It is held together by duct tape and hope.
link

couple of things to note:
the c version uses the __func__ macro to get the name of the current function. there is no equivalent in rust.
It looks like there was a proposal to work on it but it fell through.
https://github.com/rust-lang/rfcs/pull/2818

the c version uses the KBUILD_MODNAME macro to set the modname. it doesn't look like there is currently a rust equivalent. we could pass KBUILD_MODNAME to rustc as an enviroment varible to set the modname of the debug info. and we probably should make getting the modname its own dedicated rust macro.
There is a bit of a trick to get the function name: write a macro that creates a fn foo() {}, then calls core::any::type_name_of_val(foo) and then strips the trailing ::foo
I don’t think it’s super robust (like, doesn’t stop you from using it outside of a function) but I’ve used it successfully in the past

Andrew Ballance said:

It looks like there was a proposal to work on it but it fell through.
https://github.com/rust-lang/rfcs/pull/2818

Looks like this just ran out of steam. I’m sure somebody with some time could wake it up, maybe we could add it to the “wanted features” list
I tried that, but it does not work in const contexts. so it cant be used to get the function name.
Ah, darn I didn’t realize const is needed

Andrew Ballance said:

I got nerd sniped on this.
I have a somewhat working demo. It is held together by duct tape and hope.
link

couple of things to note:
the c version uses the __func__ macro to get the name of the current function. there is no equivalent in rust.
It looks like there was a proposal to work on it but it fell through.
https://github.com/rust-lang/rfcs/pull/2818

the c version uses the KBUILD_MODNAME macro to set the modname. it doesn't look like there is currently a rust equivalent. we could pass KBUILD_MODNAME to rustc as an enviroment varible to set the modname of the debug info. and we probably should make getting the modname its own dedicated rust macro.

Thanks for looking into this! Did you try booting kernel with your changes? What does the dynamic_debug/control look like now for Rust debug print?

I did boot it up in qemu and it does work as expected. I slightly modified samples/rust/rust_driver_pci.rs to act as a test.
booting with dyndbg="file rust_driver_pci.rs +plmfs" causes it to print.

[ 0.599127] rust_mod:todo:samples/rust/rust_driver_pci.rs:90: rust_driver_pci 0000:00:04.0: pci-testdev data-match count: 1

without that extra boot arg it does not print.
it shows in /proc/dynamic_debug/control as

samples/rust/rust_driver_pci.rs:90 [rust_mod]todo =pmfsl "%pA"
We can probably use module_path!() for KBUILD_MODNAME, it's not the exact same thing, but probably better?

@Andrew Ballance if you plan to work on this, could you maybe update your progress so far at https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues/453, so that we can keep things tracked? Thanks.

Another benefit of doing that is we could reference it if we want to push for the item_path!() macro.

Trevor Gross said:

Ah, darn I didn’t realize const is needed

@Gary Guo told me there is a unstable feature that makes the function constant
There is, but it’s seemingly not very close to stable unless some hard questions get answered :( (personally I think type_name should be fine even if TypeId is not, but :man_shrugging:)
(There are some pretty lengthy conversations on the rust-lang Zulip if anyone is looking for more details)

sent to the mailing list.
https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20250611202952.1670168-1-andrewjballance@gmail.com/
